name: Coffee Meringue Kisses
recipeInstructions:
These cookies - said to have been a favorite with Marie Antoinette -
are 100 percent fat-free, and have about 20 calories per cookie. They
also have no flour, so would be good for someone who isn't able to
eat wheat. They will, however, tie up your oven for 3-4 hours.
1. Preheat the oven to 250°F (121°C).
2. Use nonstick cookie sheets or line regular cookie sheets with parchment paper.
3. Process sugar and coffee powder in a food processor until very fine.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat egg whites and vanilla extract at medium speed until frothy.
5. Add cream of tartar and continue beating until soft peaks form.
6. Increase beater speed to high and gradually add the sugar-coffee mixture, beating until soft peaks form and glossy sheen develops.
7. Pipe cookies using a pastry bag fitted with a Number 6 plain round tip:
- Hold the bag upright over prepared cookie sheets and squeeze out meringue, leaving the tip buried in the batter.
- Form rounds about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
- Leave at least 1 inch between each cookie. Alternatively, drop by tablespoonfuls onto prepared sheets with 2 inches spacing between cookies.
8. Bake for 1 hour without opening the oven door. Avoid peeking after the first hour.
9. Turn off the oven and let meringues dry in the heat for an additional 2-3 hours (overnight is fine).
10. Use a spatula to remove meringues from the sheet and store them in an airtight container. Makes about 3 dozen.
